description_long: This is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with three elongated, flat blades radiating from a central hub. The fan is suspended from the ceiling by a cylindrical rod connected to a rounded mounting base. The blades are metallic and have a slightly matte, industrial finish, with no visible blade guard or protective grille. The central motor housing is compact and streamlined, with subtle ridges for structural detail. There are no visible control panels, switches, or power cords, suggesting the controls may be remote or wall-mounted. The overall appearance is utilitarian and functional, suitable for garages, workshops, or public urban spaces.
description: A ceiling-mounted electric fan with three flat metallic blades and a cylindrical suspension rod, featuring a simple, industrial design with no visible controls or blade guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three blades evenly spaced and the cylindrical suspension rod attached to the ceiling mount.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the thickness of the blades and the side profile of the central hub and rod.
description_view_2: Direct view along one blade, emphasizing the symmetry and the central motor housing.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, showing the blade arrangement and the connection between the rod and the ceiling mount.
attribute_blade_guard_design: No blade guard is present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective covering.
